text.title: SBOM Generated and Attached on Every Release
text.summary: Every release tag triggers `trivy sbom --format cyclonedx`, signs the SBOM, and uploads it to the GitHub Release as a first-class asset.

section.title: Living SBOM, not one-shot
section.p: SBOMs are now regulatory infrastructure: EU CRA reporting starts September 2026 with full enforcement December 2027, and US Executive Order 14028 already requires SBOMs for federal procurement. A "release once, ship for years" PDF is unacceptable; the SBOM must travel with the artifact.

rule.statement: Every git tag matching the release pattern (for example `v*.*.*`) MUST trigger a workflow that generates a CycloneDX SBOM with `trivy sbom --format cyclonedx`, signs the SBOM with cosign, and uploads both the SBOM and the signature to the GitHub Release as release assets. The release MUST NOT be marked non-draft until the SBOM upload step succeeds.
rule.rationale: Tying SBOM generation to the tag event guarantees the SBOM matches the exact artifact shipped, which is what auditors verify. Cosign signing prevents downstream consumers from being fed a swapped SBOM.

example.code: trivy sbom --format cyclonedx --output sbom.cdx.json . cosign sign-blob --yes sbom.cdx.json --output-signature sbom.cdx.sig gh release upload "$TAG" sbom.cdx.json sbom.cdx.sig
example.note: SBOM, signature, and upload happen as part of the tagged release pipeline.
Generated but never published or signed; auditors cannot verify the artifact-to-SBOM mapping.

antipattern.description: Generating the SBOM nightly against `main` rather than against the tagged commit.
antipattern.reason: The SBOM diverges from any released artifact between two nightly runs and is no longer admissible evidence; pin generation to the tag event.
